commit.message:
Fix denyValues to block all value references; add enforceExcludedValues for RBAC enforcement (#18)

* Fix #17: denyValues negation context + add enforceExcludedValues option

- Update validateDenyValues to track negation context during AST traversal,
  allowing NOT/!=/NOT IN comparisons with denied values to pass validation
- Add enforceExcludedValues security option that injects NOT IN filters at
  the adapter layer for server-side RBAC enforcement
- Add 22 new tests covering negation handling, double/triple negation,
  complex AND/OR expressions, and enforceExcludedValues query injection

Co-authored-by: gblikas <13577108+gblikas@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix denyValues to block all references including negated forms; update docs and tests

- Revert validateDenyValues to original behavior: block all mentions of
  denied values regardless of operator (NOT, !=, NOT IN, etc.)
- Update denyValues JSDoc to explain the explicit allow-listing design and
  when to use enforceExcludedValues instead
- Replace incorrect negation-allowing tests with tests that correctly
  expect all negated forms to throw QuerySecurityError
- Fix integration test that incorrectly expected NOT status:"archived" to
  pass denyValues validation

Co-authored-by: gblikas <13577108+gblikas@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve enforceExcludedValues tests: add field assertions and critical denyValues+enforceExcludedValues interaction test

- All existing NOT IN tests now also assert the field name ('status') to verify the exclusion targets the correct column
- Added critical test: NOT status:deleted is blocked by denyValues; NOT status:published passes but gets status NOT IN ('archived', 'deleted') injected by enforceExcludedValues, ensuring archived/deleted records are never returned

Co-authored-by: gblikas <13577108+gblikas@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comprehensive denyValues + enforceExcludedValues robustness tests

New test suite covers:
- denyValues blocking: direct equality, NOT negation for each denied value,
  compound AND/OR with denied value, deep nesting, IN with mixed/all denied values
- enforceExcludedValues injection: status:active, title query, priority:>1,
  compound AND/OR on safe fields, NOT status:published (key bypass attempt),
  separate QK instance
Each 'safe' test verifies field name ('status'), operator ('not in'), and
both excluded values ('archived', 'deleted') in the generated SQL

Co-authored-by: gblikas <13577108+gblikas@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: gblikas <13577108+gblikas@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Enforce `go fix` in CI and apply Go 1.26 modernizations (#7064)

* Enforce go fix in CI and apply modernizations

Add go fix -diff as a quality gate across all Go modules (azd core
and extensions). This ensures code stays up to date with modern Go
idioms such as interface{} -> any, range-over-int loops, maps.Copy,
slices.Contains, min/max builtins, and more.

Changes:
- Apply go fix ./... to azd core and all 6 extensions (307 files)
- Add go-fix job to reusable lint-go.yml workflow
- Add go fix -diff check to mage preflight (step 2)
- Update CONTRIBUTING.md and AGENTS.md with go fix docs
- Remove unused Ptr helpers that were inlined by go fix
- Refactor ioc/container.go to avoid go fix false positive with
  unexported types from the container package

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: JeffreyCA <jeffreyca16@gmail.com>

* Fix missing branchAndPath variable after PR review refactor

The PR review suggestion removed the branchAndPath assignment but
left a reference to it. Replace with parts[1] directly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: JeffreyCA <jeffreyca16@gmail.com>

commit.message:
ci: use orphan commits for deploy branches to reduce clone size (#1603)

* ci: use orphan commits for deploy branches to reduce clone size

Use orphan commits (single commit, no history) for gh-pages,
allure_data_history, and benchmark branches to prevent large generated
files from accumulating across hundreds of commits, which bloats the
repository pack file and slows git clone.

- deploy-website: add force_orphan: true to peaceiris/actions-gh-pages
- nightly: rewrite allure_data_history push as orphan commit
- new workflow: daily squash of continuousbenchmark branches

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* updates

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix TTL rounding to match Redis behavior (#1602)

* Fix TTL rounding to match Redis behavior (#1389)

Redis rounds TTL seconds to the nearest integer (equivalent to (ms+500)/1000),
while Garnet was truncating (flooring) via a direct (long) cast. This caused
SET+EXPIRE 10+TTL to return 9 instead of 10 when called immediately.

Fix: Use Math.Round(MidpointRounding.AwayFromZero) in both
SecondsFromDiffUtcNowTicks and MillisecondsFromDiffUtcNowTicks in
ConvertUtils.cs.

Added TtlRoundingTest to verify string and object keys return the expected
TTL value immediately after EXPIRE.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* updates

* updates

* increase test timing tolerance

* update

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix ralph loop crashes: disable mid-round restart, Oh padding, crash handler

- Changed short-phrase padding from 'Well,' to 'Oh,' (more natural for Mario)
- Expanded padding threshold to <19 chars (catches more short phrases)
- Added number normalization to clean_for_compare (one->1, percent->%)
- Fixed wmic->tasklist for Windows process killing
- Disabled mid-round subprocess restart (was killing ralph process)
- Added sys.excepthook crash handler and per-item try/except
- Updated expected texts for newly-padded phrases (#6, #16, #37)
- R48: 72.6% acceptable - new best score (32G+21O/73)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: APIエラーハンドリングの統一・強化 (#15)

- api/src/lib/errors.ts にカスタムエラークラスを追加
  - AppError（基底クラス）
  - ValidationError（400）/ NotFoundError（404）
  - UnauthorizedError（401）/ ExternalApiError（502）
- api/src/index.ts に app.onError() グローバルエラーハンドラーを追加
  - AppError は { error: { code, message } } 形式で返す
  - 予期しないエラーは console.error でログ出力し 500 を返す

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- DiscordCallbackPage.tsx を新規作成
  - /auth/discord/callback を処理するページ

  - 成功時は /repos へリダイレクト、失敗時はエラーページへ
- App.tsx に /auth/discord/callback ルートを追加

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Improve telemetry error classification with typed sentinels (#7051)

* Eliminate opaque errors_errorString from telemetry

Looked at error telemetry for azd 1.23.7 and 1.23.8. About
15k were just 'internal.errors_errorString' and ~3.6k were
'error.suggestion' — not useful for debugging.

Why this was happening:

- MapError was matching ErrorWithSuggestion wrapper before
  looking at the real error inside, so things like auth and
  ARM failures got misclassified.
- Most Run() methods used bare errors.New or fmt.Errorf with
  no typed error, so MapError had nothing to match on and
  dumped them in the catch-all.

What this does:

- Unwrap ErrorWithSuggestion first so the real error gets
  classified
- Add 28 typed errors covering all commands
- Fix all 54 bare error sites to wrap typed errors with %w
- Add ErrorWithSuggestion at each site for better agent/CLI
  experience
- Add an AST test that blocks new bare errors from being added

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove non-actionable suggestions in extensions.go

Return base errors instead of wrapping with unhelpful
'This is an internal error...' suggestion text.

Addresses JeffreyCA review feedback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert ErrorWithSuggestion unwrap logic

Restore original error.suggestion + error.type classification.
Wei confirmed the empty error.type was a backend GDPR
classification issue, now fixed. The original design is correct:
ErrorWithSuggestion produces error.suggestion as ResultCode
with the inner error type in the error.type attribute.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove dead sentinel checks and classify via ErrorWithSuggestion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: rewrite /etc/hosts via inode overwrite to bypass bind mount rename restriction

Use 'cat > /etc/hosts' instead of 'sed -i' to patch BuildKit container
hosts file. sed -i fails with 'Resource busy' on Docker bind mounts because
it tries to rename a temp file over the original. Writing via cat preserves
the inode and avoids the restriction.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement OpenViking-inspired capabilities (hotness score, used() feedback, L0/L1/L2, fast/quality paths, memory taxonomy, tool stats)

Borrowed 5 high-value capabilities from volcengine/OpenViking, maximally
leveraging openJiuwen framework built-ins (AgenticRetriever for quality path,
rrf_fusion framework already available).

New capabilities:
- Hotness Score: sigmoid(log1p(active_count)) × exp(-λ×age_days) blended
  into RRF fusion at alpha=0.2 (context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py)
- used() feedback API: POST /context/used increments active_count on
  confirmed context items, driving Hotness Score
- MemoryCategory 6-taxonomy: profile/preferences/entities/events/cases/patterns
  with AggregationRequest.category_filter support
- ContextLevel L0/L1/L2: ABSTRACT/OVERVIEW/DETAIL levels with
  AggregationRequest.max_level progressive expansion
- fast/quality dual-path: mode='quality' activates AgenticRetriever
  (openJiuwen-native) for complex tasks; mode='fast' (default) uses
  HybridRetriever for low-latency simple queries
- Tool Performance Memory: ToolContextGovernor.record_tool_result() +
  POST /tools/result endpoint; unreliable tools rank lower in select_tools()

Files changed:
- context_agent/models/context.py: +ContextLevel, +MemoryCategory enums,
  +ContextItem.{level,category,active_count,updated_at} fields
- context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py: new hotness pure function module
- context_agent/core/retrieval/search_coordinator.py: hotness-blended RRF
- context_agent/core/memory/working_memory.py: +mark_used() method
- context_agent/core/memory/tiered_router.py: +record_usage() method
- context_agent/core/retrieval/tool_governor.py: +record_tool_result(), +_sort_by_success_rate()
- context_agent/adapters/ltm_adapter.py: +agentic_search() with AgenticRetriever fallback
- context_agent/orchestration/context_aggregator.py: +mode/max_level/category_filter params
- context_agent/api/schemas.py: +ContextUsedRequest/Response, +ToolResultRequest/Response
- context_agent/api/http_handler.py: +POST /context/used, +POST /tools/result
- context_agent/api/router.py: +mark_used(), +record_tool_result() facade methods
- tests/unit/test_openviking_capabilities.py: 38 new unit tests (all passing)
- docs/architecture-design.md: +Appendix D with capability details
- docs/agent-integration-guide.md: +Section 9 with usage guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: set LogRocket dom.baseHref to restore CSS in production replays

Vite production builds extract CSS into separate hashed files under
/assets/. rrweb (used internally by LogRocket) needs a base URL to
correctly resolve and inline those relative stylesheet references
during DOM snapshot serialisation.

Without baseHref, session replays on self-hosted / private-network
deployments (e.g. http://werkstatt:3000) show the page unstyled
because LogRocket's replay servers cannot reach the private host
to fetch the CSS files.

Setting dom.baseHref to window.location.origin at runtime works for
all environments without requiring an extra env var.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial commit: Linh's Skincare Blog from Figma

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix auth mode, stable naming, ACI os-type

- Use --auth-mode login instead of account keys (key auth disabled)
- Use deterministic suffix from resource group hash (stable across runs)
- Add --os-type Linux to ACI create commands
- Enable shared key access in bench scripts (AzDataMaker needs connection strings)
- Pin generated resource names in config.env
- Fix config.env parser to handle inline comments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: multi-region-nonpaired-azurestorage
repository.description: Azure Blob Storage Object Replication demo between non-paired regions (Sweden Central → Norway East) with benchmarking

commit.message:
Fix PowerShell scripts to use --auth-mode login instead of --account-key

Storage accounts have key-based auth disabled, so all az storage
container and blob commands must use Azure AD login-based auth.

Changes across all three .ps1 files:
- Removed az storage account keys list calls
- Replaced --account-key with --auth-mode login
- Removed Key parameter from Check-BlobReplicationStatus function

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: repos.ts のコードレビュー指摘を修正

- GitHub API ページネーション対応（Linkヘッダーで全件取得）
- Cache-Control: private, no-store / Vary: Cookie ヘッダーを追加
- @seedlog/schema の Repo / ReposResponse 型を使用

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: enable God Mode tick in production with custom weather override

- /god/tick now available in production, auth via TICK_ADMIN_KEY
- Accepts optional rain_mm, wind_speed_kph, wind_direction body overrides
- use_live_weather:true fetches live then applies partial overrides
- Other god routes remain dev-only (devOnly middleware)
- UI: Tick Control panel with weather explanations, live/manual toggle,
  admin key input, result display

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: 認証フロープロトタイプ（フロントエンド + CORS対応）

APIの変更:
- hono/cors ミドルウェアを /api/* に追加（FRONTEND_URL を origin, credentials: true）

フロントエンドの追加:
- src/lib/api.ts — API クライアント（base URL + credentials: include）
- src/pages/LoginPage.tsx — GitHub ログインページ
- src/pages/GitHubCallbackPage.tsx — callback 処理（localStorageに githubLogin 保存 → /repos へ）
- src/pages/ReposPage.tsx — リポジトリ一覧 + Webhook 登録
- src/pages/ErrorPage.tsx — エラー表示
- src/App.tsx — pathname ベースのシンプルルーター

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: GET /api/repos — リポジトリ一覧取得APIを追加

フロントでリポジトリを選択して POST /api/webhooks/register に渡すために使用。
- routes/repos.ts 新規作成（署名済みCookieで認証、GitHub API GET /user/repos を呼ぶ）
- schema に repoSchema / reposResponseSchema を追加
- index.ts に /api/repos を登録
- docs/api.md に GET /api/repos を追記

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial commit: multi-region-nonpaired-azurestorage

Object replication demo between non-paired Azure regions (Sweden Central → Norway East).

Includes:
- Core scripts: create storage, enable prereqs, setup replication, cleanup
- Benchmarking scripts: AzDataMaker ingestion + replication monitoring
- Single config.env with CLI override support
- Default and priority replication modes
- README.md and publishable Blog.md
- Architecture documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
TTS v3: retry mechanism, Hmm fix, yay collapse, okee revert, duration tuning

- Added synthesis retry: up to 3 attempts, keeps longest audio
- Min duration: word_count * 0.15s (catches garbled short outputs)
- Changed Hm -> Hmm (fixes #24 producing numbers)
- Added yay+ collapse (Yayy -> yay)
- Reverted okee dokee experiment (worse than okey dokey)
- Round 32 scored 70% acceptable (new best)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

TTS cleaning v2: filler normalization, Bowzer/Gumba phonetics, short phrase padding, nocache bypass, period-comma conversion. Ralph loop R10-R24 avg 67pct acceptable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add missing workflow files from OSPO actions

Added standard workflows used across OSPO GitHub Actions:

- auto-labeler.yml: Automatically labels PRs based on release-drafter config
- copilot-setup-steps.yml: Documents Copilot CLI setup for contributors
- linter.yaml: Runs super-linter on PRs to enforce code quality
- pr-title.yml: Validates PR titles follow conventional commit format
- scorecard.yml: Runs OpenSSF Scorecard security analysis
- stale.yaml: Closes stale issues after 35 days of inactivity
- release-drafter.yml: Configuration for auto-labeler and release notes

These workflows provide:
- Automated PR labeling for release notes
- Code quality enforcement via linting
- Security scanning via Scorecard
- Stale issue management
- PR title validation

Brings pr-conflict-detector in line with other OSPO actions like
issue-metrics, stale-repos, contributors, etc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: pr-conflict-detector
repository.description: A GitHub Action to detect potential merge conflicts between open pull requests and alert the authors
